rationale: In the "Previous Models" section, the citation is tied to a specific methodological approach rather than a generic literature list. The authors use Chintagunta (2002) as an example of modeling no-purchase as an added alternative with the deterministic utility fixed at zero, then explicitly argue that this is too restrictive. They position their own model as more general because it allows richer covariate effects and correlation structures across incidence and brand choice decisions.
relation_description: The citing paper uses the cited paper as an exemplar of the add-one-outcome no-purchase specification and argues against that formulation's restrictions. It frames its own model as a superior, more flexible alternative to that approach.
